level_signals: 7+ years of product management experience on developer tools
ex-founder with strong product instincts also acceptable
Expected to independently own roadmap, prioritization, and stakeholder alignment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 7+ years of product management experience on developer tools, or an ex-founder with strong product instincts.
You're technical enough to dive in: read an architecture doc, follow a design discussion, ask the right questions.

Have a clear point of view on what makes infrastructure agent-friendly. You use AI coding tools and agent harnesses daily, track what Anthropic, OpenAI, and the AI community ship week to week, and have opinions about the primitives developers need to build agent-native products.
You've shipped something that put those opinions to the test.
Are biased toward speed. You'd rather ship something imperfect and learn than spend another sprint refining a spec. You know when to stop planning and start building.
Build with what you own. You use the products on your roadmap: connecting to Supabase through the MCP server, building with the CLI, testing how agents respond to different tool configurations.
Use AI to move faster. You use AI tools for research, drafting, and synthesising feedback.
Work async by default. Supabase is fully remote. You make decisions in writing and move threads forward without meetings.

scope.surface: MCP server, agent skills, agent evals — AI coding assistant integration layer of Supabase platform
core_responsibilities: Own the MCP server, agent skills, and evals for agent tooling
Rethink PM job when most users are agents — apply agent-first lens to product decisions
Talk to customers across the full spectrum (indie devs, startups, enterprise)
Own problem statement and requirements behind every agent tooling bet
Prioritize and defend roadmap calls across agent plugin requests, MCP improvements, discoverability, and reliability
Define launch metrics before engineering starts (developer activation, task completion, AI eval scores)
Keep engineering, design, and leadership aligned across MCP server, CLI, SDK, and dashboard

green_flags: Fully remote with global hiring — no geographic restrictions
ESOP equity for every team member
100% health insurance coverage for employees, 80% for dependents
Clear product surface and ownership (MCP server, agent skills, evals)
Async-first culture explicitly baked into role requirements
Company at significant scale ($500M raised, 7M+ registered developers)
Annual company offsite and professional development allowance
red_flags: Visa sponsorship policy not mentioned — unclear for non-US candidates despite global hiring claim
7+ years hard requirement is high bar; ex-founder alternative is vague and leaves room for inconsistent evaluation
Role scope is broad (MCP server + agent skills + evals + cross-team alignment) for a single PM

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.team_size_estimate (only company headcount of 280+ stated; immediate team size not disclosed)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities section; not listed as a discrete metrics section)
location.work_auth (inferred as open from global hiring statement; not explicitly stated)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned anywhere in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The '7+ years of product management experience on developer tools, or an ex-founder with strong product instincts' creates an ambiguous baseline — the ex-founder path has no years or domain qualifier, making severity assessment subjective. Treated as hard given it is in the primary qualifications section.
Compensation range is entirely absent; equity form is ESOP but no specifics (percentage, cliff, vesting) provided.
